description: Plotinus understood himself as a faithful interpreter of Plato — 'these are not new doctrines; they were said long ago, though not explicitly.' His entire system is a radicalisation of Platonic ideas: the Form of the Good (described by Plato as 'beyond being') becomes the One; the realm of Forms becomes Nous; the world-soul of the Timaeus becomes Soul. The hierarchical structure of reality, the soul's kinship with the intelligible world, the goal of philosophical ascent — all are Platonic. What Plotinus adds is the mystical dimension: the direct, non-cognitive union with the One as the telos of the philosophical life, and the emanation account of how the Many proceeds from the One.
source: Plotinus, Enneads V.1; Porphyry, Life of Plotinus
